title: PR-1 runtime lifecycle and Worker clock repair
summary: Repair the explicit GameSession job lifecycle and Worker-owned normal-speed scheduler so 3-tick publications expose non-instant work and a real Worker can sustain the full 600-second gate without UI-driven time.
acceptance: At unchanged normal speed and the unchanged 100 ms / 3-tick publication quantum, the authoritative PR-1 non-instant job exposes moving and working in separate coherent Worker publications, with each state present in at least two consecutive publication pairs before terminal idle

Movement and interaction progress remain explicit integer-tick JobCore state with serializable step, stepEnteredTick and stepTickCount evidence; no coroutine, Promise chain, closure-only cursor, UI clock or real clock is added to sim-core

The Worker-owned scheduler reaches at least 18000 authoritative ticks during a passive 600000 ms normal-speed run by using bounded wall-time debt/catch-up outside sim-core, while pause and speeds 0/1/2/3 retain effective 0/30/60/90 TPS semantics and shutdown/disconnect stop scheduling exactly once

Projection delivery remains latest-wins for render/UI and reliable for command, alert, save and fatal messages; focused backpressure tests prove stale snapshot drops, bounded reliable queue depth and strictly ordered reliable delivery

Fixed-seed Worker/headless world and read-model hash parity, deterministic phase order, structured lifecycle reasons and the 100000-tick zero job/reservation/queue/resource-leak gate remain passing after the duration repair
No GameSession protocol, schema, projection payload/version, save format, public compatibility promise, Web authority, test threshold or runtime dependency changes

history.details: Added already-done WM-0165 dependency, exact sim-core/sim-worker single-writer paths, unchanged 3-tick publication and 600-second/18000-tick hard gates, deterministic/backpressure checks and protocol/schema/save prohibitions. Task remains ready and unclaimed.

saveLoadStance: No save mutation or public compatibility. GameSession save/load remains unsupported and fail-closed.
forbiddenPaths: packages/sim-protocol/** and any protocol, schema, projection-version, payload, validator or public-export change
apps/**, including default Web implementation and WM-0168-owned E2E evidence
generic JobCore, reservation, pathing, store or save-format changes outside the listed GameSession repair files
changing GAME_SESSION_SCHEDULER_QUANTUM_MS=100 or GAME_SESSION_TICKS_PER_QUANTUM_AT_SPEED_1=3 to hide lifecycle visibility failures
lowering the 600000 ms, 18000 tick, two-consecutive-publication, parity, conservation or backpressure gates
UI advance helpers, drain helpers, command-drain loops, scenario runners or static fixtures as the normal product clock/authority
unbounded scheduler catch-up, sim-core wall-clock access, hot-loop allocation growth, new runtime dependencies or PR-2+ scope
executionLane: simulation-engineer only; Spark is prohibited because this repair touches Worker scheduling, deterministic lifecycle and concurrency ownership
singleWriterRule: WM-0167 is the only ready product-code writer. WM-0168 remains proposed until WM-0167 is independently verified, integrated and done; WM-0166 remains blocked.
